a: He likes cherries better than grapes.
b: He bought grapes because they were cheap.
c: He would have preferred to buy grapes.
d: He thinks cherries are less expensive.
explanation: The man implies that he would rather have bought grapes because they were cheaper.

a: She was not angry at Richard.
c: She did not understand what Richard said.

a: He is very sad about retiring.
b: He does not want to retire.
d: He has not decided to retire.
explanation: Although the sentence uses a double negative, it implies that the boss is actually pleased or satisfied about retiring.

c: Tom gave her enough money to buy a house.
explanation: The woman says Tom lent her money so she could pay her rent. This means Tom helped her financially.

b: She is probably still in the apartment.
c: She forgot to take her purse.
d: She is looking for her purse.
explanation: The man assumes Debbie is still in the apartment because her purse is still there.
